title: Take care of yourself
wikitext:
[]: "Take care of yourself"             Quote Post by FallenTabris » Mon Feb 16, 2009 8:45 pm This phrase in english sounds dismissive, almost like a friendly-but-cold goodbye. does the Japanese it's translated from bear that connotation, or is it as literal as possible?

[]:             Quote Post by Sailor Star Dust » Mon Feb 16, 2009 9:50 pm It's not translated from anything; the English and some Japanese titles of the episodes tend to come from Sci-fi novels. For example, while the English Title of Finale is "Take care of yourself", the Japanese title is "The beast that shouted love/I at the heart of the world". That aside, I personal feel the English title for Finale (Episode 26) was "Take care of yourself" is a very nice counterpart to One More Final (actual name of Episode 26'): "I need you".

[]:             Quote Post by FallenTabris » Wed Feb 25, 2009 3:53 am Oh yeah, i forgot. Well, that's good, the phrase can't lose its nuances then.

[]:             Quote Post by Axell » Wed Feb 25, 2009 12:17 pm if you say it in a loving voice its actually quite nice :)

[]:             Quote Post by Vegeta 20XX » Wed Feb 25, 2009 2:56 pm Sailor Star Dust wrote: For example, while the English Title of Finale is "Take care of yourself", the Japanese title is "The beast that shouted love/I at the heart of the world".

[]:             Quote Post by Sailor Star Dust » Wed Feb 25, 2009 3:28 pm The Perfect collection is incorrect. It's best to go with the Platinum releases since the translations and overall quality is better.

[]:             Quote Post by AyrYntake » Wed Feb 25, 2009 3:40 pm You can interpret it in different ways. Said by a person who is inevitably leaving--dying, for example--it sounds comforting and warm.

[]:             Quote Post by honsou » Wed Feb 25, 2009 5:08 pm Sailor Star Dust wrote: For example, while the English Title of Finale is "Take care of yourself", the Japanese title is "The beast that shouted love/I at the heart of the world".

[]:             Quote Post by Sailor Star Dust » Wed Feb 25, 2009 6:43 pm hons wrote: but i think in the mid point it flashed the "Take care of yourself" title.

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Wed Feb 25, 2009 7:04 pm Sailor Star Dust wrote: The Perfect collection is incorrect.

[]:             Quote Post by AyrYntake » Thu Feb 26, 2009 2:18 am It's especially evident in the use of katakana for ai instead of hiragana, which would otherwise be the more likely candidate. In reality for a common word such as love , kanji would probably be used so it's quite clear that there's a form of wordplay going on here.

[]:             Quote Post by Mr. Tines » Thu Feb 26, 2009 2:42 am The use of kana -- except in character given names -- appear to be expressly indicative of wordplay in NGE, like that movie called Angel Rebirth

[]:             Quote Post by FallenTabris » Thu Feb 26, 2009 5:47 am AyrYntake wrote: You can interpret it in different ways. Said by a person who is inevitably leaving--dying, for example--it sounds comforting and warm.

[]:             Quote Post by ZapX » Thu Feb 26, 2009 8:27 am Mr. Tines wrote: The use of kana -- except in character given names -- appear to be expressly indicative of wordplay in NGE

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Thu Feb 26, 2009 8:52 am I think he's talking about the use of katakana in titles where a perfectly normal kanji would under regular circumstances be. E.g., this instance of "AI", 最後のシ者, and シト新生.
